words.hint: A person trained to travel in a spacecraft.
A huge collection of gas, dust, and billions of stars.
A large round object that orbits a star.
A chunk of ice and rock with a glowing tail.
The curved path an object takes in space.
A shooting star burning up in the atmosphere.
A vehicle used to launch things into space.
The force that pulls objects toward the ground.
Giant balls of hot, glowing gas in the night sky.
The natural satellite that orbits the Earth.
A glass cup used by scientists for mixing liquids.
An object that attracts iron and steel.
The power needed to do work or make things move.
A state of matter like water or juice.
A state of matter that holds its own shape, like a rock.
An invisible state of matter, like the air we breathe.
To create or design something totally new.
To find the size, length, or amount of something.
A tool used to weigh things.
A tube wide at the top and narrow at the bottom.
A massive body of saltwater.
A very tall landform with a peak.
A very dry, hot, and sandy area of land.
A large natural stream of flowing water.
A piece of land completely surrounded by water.
A deep gorge, typically with a river flowing through it.
A large area covered chiefly with trees.
A low area of land between hills or mountains.
A mountain that can erupt with lava.
A spherical model of the Earth.
The framework of bones inside your body.
The muscle that pumps blood everywhere.
The organ inside your head that helps you think.
Tissue in your body that helps you move and lift things.
Two organs in your chest used for breathing.
The red liquid that carries oxygen through your veins.
Where your food goes to be digested.
Hard white objects in your mouth used for chewing.
The outer covering of your entire body.
A hard, white piece that makes up your skeleton.
A giant reptile that lived millions of years ago.
The remains of a prehistoric animal preserved in rock.
A large natural hole in the side of a hill or mountain.
When a species of animal no longer exists.
A person or animal that tracks down food.
Giant extinct elephants with long, curved tusks.
A long stick with a pointed tip used in ancient times.
A time when thick ice sheets covered parts of the Earth.
A cold-blooded animal with scales, like a lizard.
A sharp, curved nail on the foot of an animal.
A very smart and playful marine animal.
A sea creature with eight long arms.
Colorful underwater structures that look like rocks.
A fast-swimming fish with sharp teeth.
A slow-moving reptile with a hard shell.
Short for a clear, wobbly sea creature that can sting.
A tiny fish that looks like a miniature horse.
Has claws and walks sideways on the ocean floor.
The largest mammal that lives in the ocean.
You can find these empty homes on the beach.
A giant mythical creature that breathes fire.
A person who does magic with a wand.
A huge stone building where kings and queens live.
Magic words spoken to make something happen.
Tricks and illusions that seem impossible.
A tiny magical creature with wings.
A thin stick used to cast magic spells.
A brave warrior who wears shiny armor.
The male ruler of a country or castle.
The female ruler of a country or castle.
Tiny grains of rock you walk on at the beach.
The bright yellow star that makes you feel hot.
Moving water that crashes onto the shore.
You use this to dry off after swimming.
To ride on top of an ocean wave on a board.
A plastic container used to carry sand and water.
A small shovel used to dig in the sand.
Eating a packed lunch outdoors on a blanket.
Provides shade so you don't get a sunburn.
The edge of the land right next to the ocean.
The colorful part of a plant that smells nice.
A living thing that grows in the ground.
The short green blades that cover a lawn.
You plant this tiny thing to grow a new plant.
The dark soil where plants grow.
A wiggly creature that helps keep soil healthy.
Plants need sunlight and this liquid to grow.
An eight-legged bug that spins a web.
A buzzing insect that drinks nectar from flowers.
A small bug with six legs.
A crunchy orange vegetable that rabbits love.
A sweet, crunchy fruit that can be red or green.
A long yellow fruit that monkeys like to eat.
Small green or purple fruits that grow in bunches.
A round vegetable that might make you cry when chopped.
A brown vegetable used to make French fries.
A soft red fruit often used like a vegetable in salads.
Tiny green vegetables that grow inside a pod.
Yellow kernels that grow on a tall stalk.
A large, sweet, juicy fruit, like a watermelon.
A huge flying machine with wings.
A line of connected cars that travels on metal tracks.
A strong vehicle with large back wheels used on farms.
A vehicle with two wheels that you pedal.
A toy vehicle with two small wheels and a handlebar.
A flying machine with spinning blades on top.
A small vessel for traveling on water.
A large vehicle designed to carry heavy loads.
The machine part that makes a car move.
The round part of a car that rolls on the road.
The hottest season when school is out.
The coldest season, sometimes with snow.
The season when flowers begin to bloom.
The season when leaves turn orange and fall off trees.
A colorful arc in the sky after it rains.
Heavy rain with thunder and lightning.
A figure made of packed snow with a carrot nose.
When water gets so cold it turns into ice.
When the sky is gray and you can't see the sun.
A gentle, cool wind.
A game where you kick a black and white ball into a net.
A game played with a racket and a fuzzy yellow ball.
You throw the ball into this ring to score in basketball.
A popular sport played with a bat and ball on an oval field.
A round object used in many different sports.
A group of players working together to win.
The person or team that finishes first in a game.
A shiny gold, silver, or bronze award worn around the neck.
The number of points a team has in a game.
A person who is participating in a sport.
A person who helps you get better when you are sick.
A medical helper who takes care of patients in a hospital.
Officers who keep the city safe and catch bad guys.
A person who grows crops and takes care of animals.
Someone who makes bread, cakes, and cookies.
The person who flies an airplane.
Someone who helps students learn in a classroom.
A person who makes music with their voice.
Someone who paints pictures or draws.
A doctor who checks your teeth to keep them healthy.
A fabric shelter you sleep in when camping.
An outdoor fire used for warmth and roasting marshmallows.
You need logs of this to keep the fire burning.
A large area covered in tall trees.
A long walk outdoors, usually on a trail.
Strong, sturdy shoes you wear for hiking.
A dirt path through the woods.
A portable lamp used to see in the dark at camp.
You can see millions of these shining at night in the woods.
Everything outdoors, like plants, animals, and rocks.
